collections.description: Use the story index to uncover the mathematics behind each chapter, clue, and event.

items.summary: Meet Ansh before the nightmare begins and revisit the story moments that can later be connected to mathematical ideas.

items.title: The Tongue of Stone

items.title: The Smallest Spark

collections.title: Enemy Records
collections.subtitle: Antagonist Side
collections.description: Inspect antagonist systems, tasks, traps, and technology through their underlying mathematics.

items.title: The Architect
items.summary: The Architect controls connected rooms and systems as a network of nodes and routes.
items.concept: Represent locations as vertices, connections as edges, and inspect paths, cycles, and network reachability.
items.activity: Sketch the control network and find a route that avoids a monitored node.

items.title: The Calculation
items.summary: A repeating antagonist calculation grows according to a detectable numerical pattern.
items.concept: Recognize arithmetic or geometric change, determine a general term, and estimate accumulated values with a series.
items.activity: Continue the system sequence and identify the stage at which its value crosses the warning limit.

items.title: The Trap
items.summary: The trap selects outcomes under uncertainty, but its possible results can still be measured.
items.concept: List the sample space, distinguish favourable outcomes, and calculate theoretical probability.
items.activity: Compare the available escape choices and select the path with the strongest probability of success.

items.title: The Purple Orb
items.summary: The orb models a system whose calculation requires both real and imaginary components.
items.concept: Write values in a + bi form and interpret addition, multiplication, and position on the complex plane.
items.activity: Combine the orb signals and map the resulting complex number to its plane location.

collections.title: Classwise Knowledge
collections.subtitle: Boards & Grades
collections.description: Preview classwise and gradewise mathematics before the complete curriculum library is released.

items.title: Class 6 Preview
items.summary: A classwise preview of number systems, fractions, basic algebra, geometry, mensuration, and data handling.
items.concept: See how common Grade 6 foundations align across CBSE, ICSE, MP Board, and selected international pathways.
items.activity: Choose a foundation topic and compare the vocabulary and expected skill across boards.

items.title: Class 7 Preview
items.summary: A classwise preview of rational numbers, equations, ratios, geometry, exponents, and data interpretation.
items.concept: Connect shared Grade 7 outcomes while keeping board-specific sequencing and depth visible.
items.activity: Select a topic and identify which prerequisite skill should be revised first.

items.title: Class 8 Preview
items.summary: A classwise preview of linear equations, factorisation, graphs, mensuration, probability, and advanced number work.
items.concept: Compare Grade 8 topic progression across Indian boards and international grade-level expectations.
items.activity: Open a topic preview and map it to its earlier foundation and later application.

items.title: Class 9 Preview
items.summary: A classwise preview of polynomials, coordinate geometry, Euclidean geometry, statistics, and probability.
items.concept: Understand how Grade 9 concepts are grouped and assessed across boards without connecting this library to Ganith lore.
items.activity: Choose a chapter and inspect its concept map, prerequisite knowledge, and expected outcomes.

collections.title: Game Database
collections.subtitle: Rooms & Trials
collections.description: Use the game index to understand the mathematics behind rooms, rules, puzzles, and trials.

items.title: The Grid Chamber
items.summary: The chamber turns movement into positions, directions, and transformations on a grid.
items.concept: Apply coordinates, translation, reflection, and distance reasoning to navigate the room.
items.activity: Trace a valid grid route while preserving the transformation rule at every checkpoint.

items.title: The Angle Maze
items.summary: The maze responds to turns and intersections whose angle relationships reveal the correct route.
items.concept: Use complementary, supplementary, vertically opposite, and parallel-line angle relationships.
items.activity: Calculate the missing turn angles and reject paths that break the maze rule.

items.title: The Equation Lock
items.summary: Each lock segment contributes a condition that must be satisfied by the same unknown value.
items.concept: Translate the lock rules into equations and verify a solution in every original condition.
items.activity: Solve the linked equations and test the result against all lock segments.

items.title: The Probability Gate
items.summary: The gate changes among possible states, making strategy dependent on likelihood rather than certainty.
items.concept: Compare simple and conditional outcomes, then distinguish expected advantage from a guaranteed result.
items.activity: Calculate each gate option and choose the strategy with the best justified chance of opening.

quote: The more you know, the more doors you unlock. — Ganith
